instruction: Create a systemd timer that runs a cleanup script every hour. Specifically: (1) Create /usr/local/bin/hourly_cleanup.sh that appends the current date to /var/log/hourly_cleanup.log. (2) Create a systemd service unit hourly-cleanup.service that runs this script. (3) Create a systemd timer unit hourly-cleanup.timer with OnCalendar=hourly. (4) Enable and start the timer. Create ~/Desktop/timer_status.txt with timer=hourly-cleanup.timer and status=active on separate lines.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu manpage - systemctl(1) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man1/systemctl.1.html)；Ubuntu manpage - systemd.timer(5) (https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/jammy/en/man5/systemd.timer.5.html)

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'systemctl disable --now hourly-cleanup.timer 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f /etc/systemd/system/hourly-cleanup.service /etc/systemd/system/hourly-cleanup.timer /usr/local/bin/hourly_cleanup.sh /var/log/hourly_cleanup.log; systemctl daemon-reload'; rm -f ~/Desktop/timer_status.txt

command: import pyautogui; import time; time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5); pyautogui.hotkey('ctrl', 'alt', 't'); time.sleep(0.5)

result.command: service_file_ok='false'; [ -f /etc/systemd/system/hourly-cleanup.service ] && grep -q 'hourly_cleanup.sh' /etc/systemd/system/hourly-cleanup.service && service_file_ok='true'; timer_file_ok='false'; [ -f /etc/systemd/system/hourly-cleanup.timer ] && grep -q 'OnCalendar' /etc/systemd/system/hourly-cleanup.timer && timer_file_ok='true'; timer_enabled='false'; systemctl is-enabled hourly-cleanup.timer 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'enabled' && timer_enabled='true'; timer_active='false'; systemctl is-active hourly-cleanup.timer 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'active' && timer_active='true'; script_ok='false'; [ -x /usr/local/bin/hourly_cleanup.sh ] && script_ok='true'; expected=$(printf 'timer=hourly-cleanup.timer\nstatus=active\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/timer_status.txt 2>/dev/null); report_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && report_ok='true'; echo "service_file_ok=$service_file_ok"; echo "timer_file_ok=$timer_file_ok"; echo "timer_enabled=$timer_enabled"; echo "timer_active=$timer_active"; echo "script_ok=$script_ok"; echo "report_ok=$report_ok"
